preamble: The transformation from an alveolar flap to a voiced alveolar fricative is a process known as assibilation or zetacism, where a rhotic sound is raised or tightened until it creates friction.

examples.note: Historical shift where the rhotic assibilated into a voiced sibilant in most Turkic languages (Zetacism).
In Northern Vietnamese dialects, the historical rhotic has shifted to a voiced alveolar sibilant.
In some dialects (Andean, Mexican, Costa Rican), rhotics undergo assibilation, sounding like a buzzing sibilant.

sources: Johanson, L., & Csató, É. Á. (1998). The Turkic Languages.
Alves, M. J. (2006). A Grammar of Northern Vietnamese.
